programmes.show_description: Children across North America interact with ghosts that their parents can't see and citizens in China fear the worst as hundreds of orbs swarm in the moonlight. (S8, ep 2)
UFO Over the Andes, the Ohio Grassman, and Ancient Horn Mystery: Did a Chilean family photograph a real UFO over the Andes? And does a huge fossil point to a prehistoric figure? (S4, ep 2)
Men in Black Calls, Old Kentucky Aliens, and Mysterious Eclipse: What is the identity of a bizarre creature spotted on a driveway? And did the Men in Black threaten a man? (S4, ep 3)
Falcon 9 Mystery, Demonic Wall Climber and Leningrad Alien Fetus: Are reports of a strange swirling vortex with a brilliant white center in the skies proof of a genuine UFO? (S4, ep 9)
Elizabethan Plymouth: Alice explores the stories of England's 'Golden Age', from Sir Francis Drake to the Spanish Armada, piracy, theft, and the slave trade. (S3, ep 4)
Portsmouth Age Of Empire: In Portsmouth, Alice Roberts learns about Nelson's dark side, the heat of the battle at Trafalgar, the role of the Navy in the British Empire, and more. (S3, ep 3)
Wolverhampton 2: The dealers come across a music box, and some letters from former prime ministers Edward Heath and Harold Macmillan land on Stewart Hogfather's table. (S10, ep 25)
Darting and Dodging: Rick and Corey dart out of the shop to check out a 1968 convertible. Meanwhile, Chum desperately wants a piece of an oreodont fossil. (S24, ep 15)
...Henry's Wreck-trospective. The Fairford Wreck-Trospective: Dive headfirst into the archives to celebrate the very best bits from the hit series Junk & Disorderly. (S1, ep 6)
A Ricky Runs Through It: Victor battles to end his losing streak in Waxahachie, where some buyers prove that playing it tough is the only way to make a killing. (S3, ep 3)
Mayor Of Money Town: Victor gets more than he bargained for when he pursues a motorcycle in Carrollton. Ricky finds himself face to face with one of his biggest fears. (S4, ep 5)
This Can't Be: Brandon and Lori take a risk on a large covered item during an unusual auction. Will the reveal rev their profits or stall their cash flow? (S2, ep 10)
Swollen Profits: A locker unleashes a swarm of more than just merchandise while Jesse throws down to win antiques. (S2, ep 11)
Workmen in Canada film an enormous and enraged humanoid figure appearing to throw a tree trunk in the forests of Alberta. (S4, ep 2)
Residents of a village reported seeing a statue of the Virgin Mary move, but some believe the phenomenon was just an optical illusion or a prank. (S1, ep 5)
Millions mourned the death of Elvis Presley in 1977, but he's been spotted hundreds of times since. (S1, ep 8)
People reveal details from their past lives as celebrities, while experts investigate evidence that suggests death may not be the end of our existence. (S1, ep 9)
In 1977, schoolchildren spot a cigar-shaped aircraft in Wales. The sighting transforms the quiet seaside town into a UFO hot spot. (S1, ep 11)
Alleged sightings of leprechauns and fairies are nothing new in Ireland. Experts debate whether these Celtic creatures are fact or fiction. (S1, ep 13)
Experts investigate why religious apparitions appear on seemingly normal objects to seemingly normal people. Is it blind faith or supernatural phenomena? (S1, ep 17)
Believers and skeptics debate the origins and meanings of the mysterious formations known as crop circles. (S1, ep 20)
Experts investigate if a series of house fires across the UK are linked to an allegedly cursed portrait. (S1, ep 22)
A strange creature was reportedly seen in a wooded area of Massachusetts by different people, all giving similar descriptions. Coincidence, collusion, or a genuine mystery? (S1, ep 24)
What is the Voynich Manuscript, and what secrets does the mysterious 500-year-old document hold? (S1, ep 26)
Antiquities expert Timothy Smith looks into the existence of a code hidden in the world's oldest complete text of the Hebrew Bible, which some believe to be the actual word of God.
This Can't Be: Brandon and Lori take a risk on a large covered item during an unusual auction. Will the reveal rev their profits or stall their cash flow? (S2, ep 10)
Swollen Profits: A locker unleashes a swarm of more than just merchandise while Jesse throws down to win antiques. (S2, ep 11)
A Ricky Runs Through It: Victor battles to end his losing streak in Waxahachie, where some buyers prove that playing it tough is the only way to make a killing. (S3, ep 3)
Mayor Of Money Town: Victor gets more than he bargained for when he pursues a motorcycle in Carrollton. Ricky finds himself face to face with one of his biggest fears. (S4, ep 5)
Wolverhampton 3: Items of interest include a book about the Titanic which arrives on David Tupman's table, while Karen gets a little too carried away with a cavalry sword. (S10, ep 26)
Pushing Boundaries: When the team deploys a cutting-edge new technology on Skinwalker Ranch to study the mysterious Bubble, it leads to an unsettling revelation. (S6, ep 5)
Purgatory Bridge: Andy and Paul return to the Ranch, at the bridge that has been dubbed The Miller Triangle and find more evidence that the two sites are linked. (S3, ep 5)
Building a Super Power: Witness the truth behind the Ancient Egyptian superpower we know today as experts dive into the history of their iconic monuments and strong leaders. (S2, ep 1)
Death & the Afterlife: Explore the Ancient Egyptians' obsession with death and the afterlife by using cutting-edge new science to expose secret artefacts and rituals. (S2, ep 2)
The Mystery Of Nazca: Giorgio Tsoukalos heads to Peru to investigate whether the strange symbols covering its desert floor are key to proving aliens once visited Earth. (S1, ep 9)
The Linda Moulton Howe Files: For over 40 years, award-winning journalist Linda Moulton Howe has covered mysterious phenomena - which could lead us to a profound new reality. (S16, ep 18)
From Truman to Trump, there has been a legacy of secrecy and controversy surrounding UFOs and the US presidency. Could these powerful leaders be hiding the truth from the public?
Inside Job: When the Golds uncover a series of bogus pawns, their investigation leads to a trusted employee and a confession. (S12, ep 8)
The Hot Rod: Les and Seth visit a customer with a garage full of amazing vintage cars. Against his kids' wishes, Les offers big money for one of the seller's golden oldies. (S12, ep 9)
Ashley in Charge: With Les and Seth away at the Autorama car show - and Ashley in charge of the store - the employees go a little crazy. (S12, ep 10)
Join antiques expert and presenter David Dickinson as more collectors try to sell their items to dealers. Will they strike a deal or take a chance at auction instead? (S9, ep 46)
Wolverhampton 3: Items of interest include a book about the Titanic which arrives on David Tupman's table, while Karen gets a little too carried away with a cavalry sword. (S10, ep 26)
Battle-Pawn: Rick prepares for battle over a pair of vintage posters, Corey takes a look at an old fireman's megaphone and Chum heads over to a photo gallery. (S24, ep 16)
...Henry's Wreck-trospective. The Ardingly Wreck- Trospective: Dive headfirst into the archives to celebrate the very best bits from the hit series Junk & Disorderly. (S1, ep 7)
The Ninja And The Pitmaster: A stranger on a motorbike raises prices and eyebrows at in DeSoto. With just four lockers up for grabs, the bidding wars are fiercer than ever. (S4, ep 7)
Throw Momma From The Auction: Moe gets on the wrong side of Mary when he buys a risky locker in Lancaster. Jenny causes a stir when she brings her mother to the auction. (S4, ep 9)
Business in a Box: The buyers come face to face with one of the strangest lockers ever seen, before a surprise sends a winning bidder through the roof. (S2, ep 12)
Hold Tight & Bid High: Buyers go head to head at an airport auction, Jesse gambles on a mystery box and Scott and Chrissy battle it out over a jet-fuelled jewel. (S2, ep 13)
Cameras capture footage of millions of strange creatures invading a church and some wonder if it's evidence of a grisly buried secret. (S4, ep 4)
Thousands of UFO sightings and abductions are reported every year. This series examines these accounts and casts new light on historic cases. (S1, ep 1)
Pushing Boundaries: When the team deploys a cutting-edge new technology on Skinwalker Ranch to study the mysterious Bubble, it leads to an unsettling revelation. (S6, ep 5)
Darting and Dodging: Rick and Corey dart out of the shop to check out a 1968 convertible. Meanwhile, Chum desperately wants a piece of an oreodont fossil. (S24, ep 15)
Hestered in the Highlands: The buyers head to the hills of Murietta for an auction that has tensions and prices running high. Dave drives Darrell to breaking point. (S7, ep 10)
Auction Boogaloo: Fierce competition greets Rene and Casey as they return to the auctions. Dave adds to the heat before unearthing a fantastic piece of board game history. (S7, ep 11)
Henry, Skid and Allen splash the fun fund on a forgotten Raleigh Grifter and race to revive it for one last big finish. Season finale. (S4, ep 10)
The Top Ten Alien Influencers: Did a biblical patriarch receive instructions from 200 angels, and was Egypt's most controversial Pharaoh an extraterrestrial being? (S16, ep 1)
The Alien Code: Giorgio A Tsoukalos investigates famous mysteries from every corner of the globe. Could they share links that connect them to the extraterrestrial? (S1, ep 10)
Business in a Box: The buyers come face to face with one of the strangest lockers ever seen, before a surprise sends a winning bidder through the roof. (S2, ep 12)
Hold Tight & Bid High: Buyers go head to head at an airport auction, Jesse gambles on a mystery box and Scott and Chrissy battle it out over a jet-fuelled jewel. (S2, ep 13)
The Ninja And The Pitmaster: A stranger on a motorbike raises prices and eyebrows at in DeSoto. With just four lockers up for grabs, the bidding wars are fiercer than ever. (S4, ep 7)
Throw Momma From The Auction: Moe gets on the wrong side of Mary when he buys a risky locker in Lancaster. Jenny causes a stir when she brings her mother to the auction. (S4, ep 9)
Wolverhampton 4: Has David Tupman got his work cut out when a Ruskin vase turns up? Will Karen Dalmeny be able to afford some beautiful Royal Worcester plates? (S10, ep 27)
Henry, Skid and Allen splash the fun fund on a forgotten Raleigh Grifter and race to revive it for one last big finish. Season finale. (S4, ep 10)
Attack of the Semi: Tempers flare when a dump truck driver clashes with an angry motorist over an exhaust, turning a minor dispute into a heated standoff. (S1, ep 11)
Highway Robbery: Road rage escalates as a Pennsylvania taxi driver faces a gunman and a Texas motorist threatens others with an axe. (S1, ep 12)
Enemy Transformed: Hunters return to the Everglades to find the enemy transformed, while giant female pythons learn to hide from their pursuers. (S4, ep 1)
